complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Finalize Sanity Schemas and Populate Initial Content
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Based on the detailed implementation and testing plan for Task 16, expand it into granular subtasks. The subtasks should cover: 1. Modifying all specified Sanity schema files. 2. Refactoring all corresponding GROQ queries and frontend components in the Next.js app to match the new schema names. 3. Populating the foundational `talentPoolCategory` documents. 4. Populating a representative set of sample content for `post`, `event`, `guide`, and `exhibitionGame`. 5. Linking the newly created content via reference fields. 6. Performing a full end-to-end validation of both the data queries and the frontend presentation.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: The complexity is rated 7/10 due to the high-risk nature of modifying existing data schemas that are already consumed by a built frontend. Renaming fields requires coordinated changes in both the CMS schema and all frontend queries/components that use them, introducing a significant risk of breaking changes. The task also involves a broad scope, covering 6 different content types, and requires not just schema changes but also the creation of a comprehensive and realistic initial content set to support full application testing. The testing strategy itself is extensive, requiring both data-layer (GROQ) and presentation-layer validation.
